_comment: 20 golden-set fixtures for the nuance scoring calibration harness (docs/specs/E1-rubric-golden-set.md). Every fixture is builder-authored and PROVISIONAL: the operator hand-scores the finals Jul 11-12 and removes the `provisional` flag as part of that pass. See docs/NUANCE_RUBRIC.md for the full rubric these fixtures exercise. Rules transcribed verbatim from ARCHITECTURE.md §5.1.3.

_schema.answers: array of {question_id, response_type: 'tap'|'complicated'|'structured', position?, other_side?} — the exact nuance_sessions.answers jsonb shape
_schema.expected_score: int — sum of per-question scores this fixture must produce
_schema.rationale: one-line why this expected_score is correct under the rubric
_schema.provisional: true for every builder-authored fixture; the operator's Jul 11-12 pass replaces content and deletes this key/sets it false

answers.position: yes

fixtures.rationale: Bare Yes tap scores 1 point per the rubric's first rule.

answers.position: no

fixtures.rationale: Bare No tap scores 1 point — same rule as gs-01, opposite direction, to prove the scorer doesn't special-case yes vs no.
"It's complicated" tap scores 2 points per the rubric's second rule. Required by spec DoD ("complicated tap").

answers.position: Term limits would force out good leaders along with bad ones over time.
answers.other_side: Career politicians build up expertise that new people simply cannot match.

fixtures.rationale: Clean 3-point case: both fields well over 40 chars (73 and 76) and clearly distinct in content (trigram similarity ~0.03, far below the 0.55 near-duplicate threshold).

answers.position: Curfews unfairly limit teenage freedom.
answers.other_side: Some teens break curfew but most families already follow reasonable house rules.

fixtures.rationale: 40-char boundary, fails side: position is exactly 39 chars (one under the >=40 minimum), so the structured attempt falls back to the 2-point tier even though other_side is long and clearly not a duplicate. Isolates the char-count rule from the trigram rule.

answers.position: Curfews unfairly limits teenage freedom.
answers.other_side: Some teens break curfew but most families already follow reasonable house rules.

fixtures.rationale: 40-char boundary, passes side: position is exactly 40 chars (meets the >=40 minimum exactly), other_side is long and distinct (trigram similarity ~0.11) -> qualifies for 3. Same companion other_side as gs-05/gs-07 so length is the only variable changing across the three.

answers.position: A curfew unfairly limits teenage freedom.
answers.other_side: Some teens break curfew but most families already follow reasonable house rules.

fixtures.rationale: 40-char boundary, one-over side: position is exactly 41 chars, comfortably past the minimum -> qualifies for 3. Completes the 39/40/41 boundary triad required by the spec DoD.

answers.position: I think city curfews for teenagers do more harm than good in most neighborhoods.

fixtures.rationale: Empty other_side on a structured attempt: position alone is not "both sides" (spec DoD explicitly requires this case to score 2, not 3), even though position itself is long and thoughtful.

answers.position: Term limits would force accountability into the political system by requiring new leadership after a set number of years in office; supporters say this prevents career politicians from entrenching themselves.
answers.other_side: Term limits would force accountability into the political system by requiring new leadership after a set number of years in office; critics say this wastes valuable experience right when officials finally understand the job.

fixtures.rationale: Near-duplicate pair JUST ABOVE the proposed trigram threshold (similarity 0.5683 > 0.55): both fields share a long common clause, differing only in the final claim. Both fields individually exceed 40 chars, but the near-duplicate rule caps this at 2 points. See docs/NUANCE_RUBRIC.md 'Trigram threshold' for the full derivation.

answers.position: Term limits would force accountability into the political system by requiring new leadership after some years; supporters say this prevents career politicians from entrenching themselves.
answers.other_side: Term limits would force accountability into the political system by requiring new leadership after some years; critics say this wastes valuable experience right when officials finally understand the job.

fixtures.rationale: Near-duplicate boundary pair JUST BELOW the proposed trigram threshold (similarity 0.5297 < 0.55): same construction as gs-09 with a shorter shared clause, tuned so the pair falls just under the line and legitimately qualifies for 3. gs-09/gs-10 together are the boundary demonstration required by the spec DoD.

answers.position: asdkfj alsdkfj alskdjf laskjdf laksjdf laksjdlfkzz qq
answers.other_side: zzzxcv mnbvcxz poiuytre qwaszx mmm nnn kkk jjj hhh gg

fixtures.rationale: DOCUMENTED LIMITATION: gibberish/keyboard-mash text that happens to clear both the 40-char minimum and the trigram near-duplicate check scores 3 mechanically, because the scorer makes no quality judgment (only structure). This is by design (ARCHITECTURE §5.1.3): the `excluded` flag + 10% admin spot-check (N6/N8), not the scorer, is the intended backstop for low-effort text. Required by spec DoD ('gibberish that mechanically scores 3').

answers.position: yes
Charter schools should be funded the same as traditional public schools per pupil.
answers.other_side: Diverting funds to charters can strip resources from the public schools most students still attend.

answers.position: no

fixtures.rationale: Realistic 5-question baseline session with a mix of response types: sums to 1 + 2 + 3 + 1 + 2 = 9. Exercises score() summing across a multi-question answers array, not just a single question.

answers.position: yes
no
yes
yes
no

fixtures.rationale: Floor of the 5-question baseline: all five questions answered with a bare Yes/No tap -> 1 point each, total 5 (the minimum possible session score).

answers.position: Standardized testing gives an objective, comparable measure of student learning across schools.
answers.other_side: Standardized tests often measure test-taking skill and access to prep resources more than real learning.

answers.position: Voting should be mandatory to make elected government reflect the entire population, not just motivated voters.
answers.other_side: Compelling people to vote can produce careless or random ballots from people who would rather not participate.

answers.position: A four-day work week would improve wellbeing without much loss in real output for most jobs.
answers.other_side: Many employers believe a four-day week would simply compress the same workload into more stressful days.

answers.position: Social media platforms should verify the identity of every account to reduce harassment and bots.
answers.other_side: Mandatory ID verification would endanger whistleblowers, abuse survivors, and other users who need anonymity.

answers.position: Cities should prioritize bike lanes over car lanes on most downtown streets.
answers.other_side: Removing car lanes downtown can hurt small businesses and residents who depend on driving.

fixtures.rationale: Ceiling of the 5-question baseline: all five questions answered with a qualifying structured response (each pair well over 40 chars and clearly non-duplicate) -> 3 points each, total 15 (the maximum possible session score).

answers.other_side: Renewable subsidies help new technologies reach the scale where they can compete on price alone.

fixtures.rationale: Symmetric case to gs-08: empty position with a long other_side still falls back to 2. Confirms the rule is 'both fields', not 'other_side specifically' or 'position specifically'.

A 'structured' response_type submitted with both fields empty (e.g. user opened the structured form and closed it without typing) still scores 2, same as any other failed structured attempt -- never 1, since the response_type recorded is 'structured', not 'tap'.

answers.position: Zoning laws restrict what property owners can build on their own land without city approval.
answers.other_side: Zoning laws restrict what property owners can build on their own land without city approval.

fixtures.rationale: Literal copy-paste: other_side is byte-identical to position (trigram similarity = 1.0, the obvious extreme of the near-duplicate rule). Both fields are long, but this is definitionally not 'both sides' -> 2 points.

answers.other_side: This is a plain sentence over forty characters long for comparison.

fixtures.rationale: UNICODE EDGE CASE (flagged ambiguous, see handoff): position is 40 emoji code points (U+1F642, each a UTF-16 surrogate pair). reference-scorer counts by code point (Array.from) so this reads as exactly 40 chars and qualifies. Whether Postgres char_length()/pg_trgm agree on code-point counting for astral characters is unverified against a real B4 implementation -- see handoff ambiguous-fixture list.

answers.position: yes
Universal basic income could reduce poverty without the stigma attached to targeted welfare programs.
answers.other_side: Funding a universal basic income at a meaningful level would require large tax increases or cuts elsewhere.

answers.position: no

fixtures.rationale: A day-30 session (as opposed to baseline) with the same mixed-type shape as gs-12, scoring identically (9). Confirms score() depends only on `answers`, never on `kind`/`elapsed_days` -- those are session metadata, not scoring inputs.

answers.position: Homework beyond a modest amount does little to improve learning for most students.
answers.other_side: Regular homework builds study habits and gives teachers a window into which students are struggling.

answers.position: Homework beyond a modest amount does little to improve learning for most students in any grade.
answers.other_side: Homework beyond a modest amount does little to help learning for most students in any grade level.

answers.position: yes

fixtures.rationale: Realistic 5-question session mixing a qualifying structured pair (q2, 3 pts) with a near-duplicate structured pair (q3: near-paraphrase of itself, clearly a near-duplicate at similarity well above 0.55, 2 pts) alongside taps/complicated -- exercises the near-duplicate rule inside a multi-question sum: 2 + 3 + 2 + 1 + 2 = 10.
